King & Spalding LLP ("King & Spalding"), legal counsel to the Company

bid_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest. The bids received in the preliminary indications of interest ranged from $20.00 per share to $22.00 per share.

On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six. The bid submitted by Party X ranged from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.

On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven. The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

event_claims.description: Board first discussed exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: At a June 20, 2013 meeting, our board of directors first discussed the possibility of exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.

event_claims.description: Board unanimously agreed to proceed with initial steps related to exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously agreed to proceed with certain initial steps related to exploring our strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.

event_claims.description: Board decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.
event_claims.quote_text: At a January 28, 2014 board meeting, our board of directors discussed our year to date performance, our anticipated performance against the fiscal year 2014 plan and various factors that could impact our future performance. Following discussions, our board of directors decided to engage in a process to consider strategic alternatives.

event_claims.description: Target board approved engagement letter for BofA Merrill Lynch to act as financial advisor for strategic alternatives review.
event_claims.quote_text:
Following such discussions, our board of directors unanimously approved an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch providing for

BofA Merrill Lynch to act as our financial advisor in connection with reviewing our strategic alternatives.

event_claims.description: Board approved moving forward with contacting potential buyers, executing confidentiality agreements, and holding meetings.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously approved moving forward with next steps, including contacting the potential buyers on the list discussed by our board of directors, executing confidentiality agreements with such parties and conducting meetings with such parties.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch contacted fifty potential buyers at the board's direction.
event_claims.quote_text: During the next several weeks, BofA Merrill Lynch, at the direction of our board of directors, contacted fifty potential buyers (comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers, including financial buyers who owned businesses similar to our businesses), and we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.

event_claims.description: Company entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital.
event_claims.quote_text: We entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital on March 19, 2014.

event_claims.description: Management delivered marketing materials and held an introductory meeting with New Mountain Capital representatives.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 20, 2014, management delivered the marketing materials and held an introductory meeting with representatives of New Mountain Capital.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter with preliminary indications of interest due by April 14, 2014.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 27, 2014, at the direction of our board of directors, BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first round process letter, which informed potential buyers that non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due no later than April 14, 2014.

event_claims.description: Five parties submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.

event_claims.description: Board reviewed the five preliminary indications of interest and determined to continue the process with those parties.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors reviewed the five preliminary indications of interest that had been received. After further discussion, our board of directors determined to continue the process with the parties who had submitted preliminary indications of interest.

event_claims.description: Party X submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six.

event_claims.description: Party X informed BofA Merrill Lynch it was no longer interested in pursuing a transaction.
event_claims.quote_text:
However, on

30
<!-- PAGE 38 -->
ZEQ.=1,SEQ=37,EFW="2224840",CP="ZEP INC.",DN="1",CHK=270609,FOLIO='30',FILE='DISK116:[15ZBF1.15ZBF74401]CK74401A.;29',USER='CHE106206',CD='21-MAY-2015;09:35'

May 14, 2014, prior to receiving access to the electronic data room or meeting with the Company's management, Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.

event_claims.description: Party Y submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest, bringing total preliminary indications to seven.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven.

event_claims.description: Draft merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 22, 2014, a draft of the merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders (Party X having withdrawn from the process on May 14, 2014) that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.

event_claims.actor_label: five of the remaining six interested parties
event_claims.description: Five of the remaining six interested parties said they were unable to proceed with the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Over the next few weeks, five of the remaining six interested parties communicated to representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that they were unable to proceed with the process due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities.

event_claims.description: The sixth remaining interested party did not respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's transaction communications.
event_claims.quote_text: The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.

event_claims.description: Board decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: At a June 26, 2014 meeting of our board of directors, based on the lack of buyer interest and the uncertainty surrounding the impact of the fire at our aerosol manufacturing facility in Marietta, Georgia, our board of directors decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time and to continue to focus on our business and financial performance.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to acquire the Company for $19.25 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

event_claims.description: New Mountain Capital indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC.
event_claims.quote_text: The indication of interest was supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, contained a request for a forty-five day exclusivity period and contemplated a "go-shop" period following the signing of any definitive merger agreement.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: During the next several weeks, BofA Merrill Lynch, at the direction of our board of directors, contacted fifty potential buyers (comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers, including financial buyers who owned businesses similar to our businesses), and we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.

On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.

On May 7, 2014, access to an electronic data room containing diligence materials was made available to the five parties who submitted preliminary indications of interest to that point and these parties engaged in extensive due diligence investigations of our business.

On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six.
On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven.

On May 22, 2014, a draft of the merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders (Party X having withdrawn from the process on May 14, 2014) that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
